channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok Notes is shutting down as Lemon8 steps in
item.description: TikTok is shutting down TikTok Notes—wait, you didn’t even know it existed? Well, that explains a lot. TikTok Notes, the platform’s short-lived attempt to take on Instagram (just as Instagram Reels was built to mimic TikTok), is officially being retired.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: DHS plans to slash HQ workforce, including AI experts
item.description: DHS is planning sweeping workforce cuts across its Management Directorate, with targets including the AI Corps and the CX Directorate. The post DHS plans to slash HQ workforce, including AI experts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon and OnlyFans founder place bids on TikTok ahead of imminent deadline for a U.S. buyer
item.description: As the weekend deadline for TikTok to find a buyer approaches, bidders for the short-video social media site are piling up. Amazon and, separately, a consortium led by OnlyFans founder Tim Stokely are the latest to throw their hats into the ring for TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: AI PCs: A paradigm shift in the modern workplace
item.description: The modern workplace is undergoing the most transformative era since the dawn of the internet, with AI PCs emerging as a driving force in redefining productivity, security, and enterprise IT management.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Rust language adds trait upcasting
item.description: The Rust team has announced Rust 1.86, an update of the language that now features trait upcasting, or the ability to upcast trait objects. Rust 1.86 was announced April 3.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Visa unveils a trio of new tools to make the payments process easier
item.description: At Visa’s ETA Transact event on April 3, the payments giant introduced three new products designed to simplify and secure payment acceptance. These innovations—Authorize.
item.dc:creator: Lily McDonald, Fast Company

item.title: Relentless hackers abandon Windows to target your Apple ID
item.description: Experts say hackers who used to focus on Windows operating systems are increasingly targeting Apple IDs as part of a new phishing campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge’s rumored delay could be due to technical issues, and there’s a chance it won’t launch at all
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge's future looks in doubt, with further testing apparently needed and the possibility that it could be canceled.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Humanoid robot stuns with perfect side-flip acrobatics
item.description: Unitree, a Chinese robotics company that developed a backflipping robot, has now introduced a humanoid robot capable of doing a side flip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta is fined by Turkey after refusing to restrict content on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Meta said it has been hit with a hefty fine for resisting Turkish government demands to limit content on Facebook and Instagram.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Analysts: Trump tariffs 'worse than the worst case scenario' for tech investors
item.description: Analysts described President Trump’s latest slate of tariffs Wednesday as “worse than the worst case scenario” for tech investors.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Judge blocks social media age-verification law in Arkansas
item.description: A federal judge in Arkansas permanently blocked a law requiring age verification for social media accounts, deeming it unconstitutional. The law was stopped by U.S. District Judge Timothy L. Brooks of the Western District of Arkansas.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: IBM Cloud speeds AI workloads with Intel Gaudi 3 accelerators
item.description: IBM Cloud is broadening its AI technology services with Intel Gaudi 3 AI accelerators now available to enterprise customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump launches new office to oversee CHIPS Act, boost semiconductor investments
item.description: US President Donald J. Trump signed an executive order this week creating the US Investment Accelerator office to oversee the CHIPS and Science Act, a Biden-era program to re-shore semiconductor production.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump to hold a meeting on possible investors to buy TikTok with possible ban at stake
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump will hold a Wednesday meeting with aides about possible investors who could buy a stake in TikTok, a deal that could potentially stop the social media site from being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon, OpenAI, and China’s Zhipu unveil new AI tools amid intensifying competition
item.description: A wave of new AI products is hitting the market, signaling a shift toward more autonomous, task-completing systems that could reshape how businesses and consumers interact with digital services. Amazon has unveiled Nova Act, an AI agent designed to operate a web browser much like a human user.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Python lock file format will specify dependencies
item.description: Python’s builders have accepted a proposal to create a universal lock file format for Python projects that would specify dependencies, enabling installation reproducibility in a Python environment.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple Introduces Swiftly 1.0: A Version Manager for the Swift Programming Language
item.description: Swiftly 1.0 is a version manager for Swift that makes it easier to install, manage, and update the Swift tool chain. Apple has officially supported it as part of the core Swift tool chain.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Dow wants to power its Texas manufacturing complex with new nuclear reactors instead of natural gas
item.description: Dow, a major producer of chemicals and plastics, wants to use next-generation nuclear reactors for clean power and steam at a Texas manufacturing complex instead of natural gas. Dow's subsidiary, Long Mott Energy, applied Monday to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission for a construction permit.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What one giant industry likes and dislikes about DOGE
item.description: The Consumer Technology Association is a big industry that has mixed feelings about the Trump-Musk-DOGE approach to the government. The post What one giant industry likes and dislikes about DOGE first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Jack Black, Jason Momoa enter the Minecraft universe as the video game phenom hits the big screen
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When Jack Black got the call to star in the “Minecraft” movie, the perks were plenty. He got to work alongside Jason Momoa, reunite with “Nacho Libre” director Jared Hess and engulf himself into a beloved gaming universe.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DoD Modernization Exchange 2025: Savan Kong on building first Pentagon CX Office from the ground up
item.description: DoD’s former CXO shares details from the young office’s efforts to begin scaling customer experience across the department. The post DoD Modernization Exchange 2025: Savan Kong on building first Pentagon CX Office from the ground up first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: China's telecoms giant Huawei Technologies says its profit fell 28% while revenue surged in 2024
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Chinese telecoms gear giant Huawei said Monday its net profit fell 28% in 2024 while its revenue surged, as the company invested heavily in advanced technologies.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
